name: Memory (TencentDB)
description: Four-layer memory system — auto-captures, structures, and profiles conversational knowledge

storeBackend.description: Storage backend: sqlite (local SQLite + sqlite-vec) or tcvdb (Tencent Cloud VectorDB)

enabled.description: Whether to enable automatic conversation capture

excludeAgents.description: Agent glob patterns to exclude (for example, bench-judge-*); matching agents are not captured, recalled, or scheduled in the pipeline

l0l1RetentionDays.description: Number of days to keep local L0/L1 files. 0 = no cleanup; nonzero values must normally be >= 3. Values of 1 or 2 require allowAggressiveCleanup to be explicitly enabled

allowAggressiveCleanup.description: Whether to allow high-risk cleanup settings (l0l1RetentionDays = 1 or 2)

enableDedup.description: Intelligent L1 deduplication (detects conflicts using vector similarity or keywords)

maxMemoriesPerSession.description: Maximum number of memories extracted per session in a single L1 run

model.description: Model used for extraction (format: provider/model). Uses the OpenClaw default model when omitted

persona.description: Scene summarization and user persona settings (L2/L3)

triggerEveryN.description: Generate a persona after every N new memories

backupCount.description: Number of persona backups to keep

sceneBackupCount.description: Number of scene-block backups to keep

model.description: Model used for persona generation (format: provider/model). Uses the OpenClaw default model when omitted

everyNConversations.description: Trigger L1 batch processing every N conversation turns

enableWarmup.description: Warm-up mode: new sessions start by triggering after 1 turn, then double after each L1 run (1→2→4→...→everyN) to speed up early memory extraction

l1IdleTimeoutSeconds.description: L1 idle timeout in seconds: how long after the user stops talking to trigger L1 batch processing

l2DelayAfterL1Seconds.description: Delay in seconds after L1 completes before triggering L2

l2MinIntervalSeconds.description: Minimum interval in seconds between two L2 extractions for the same session

l2MaxIntervalSeconds.description: Maximum L2 polling interval in seconds for the same active session

sessionActiveWindowHours.description: Session active window in hours; sessions inactive longer than this stop L2 polling

strategy.description: Search strategy: keyword, embedding, or hybrid (mixed RRF fusion, recommended)

timeoutMs.description: Overall recall timeout in milliseconds; on timeout, memory injection is skipped and a warning is logged

enabled.description: Whether to enable vector search (disabled in practice when provider=none)

provider.description: Embedding service provider: enter the name of a remote OpenAI-compatible service (for example, openai or deepseek); leave empty or set to none to disable vector search

proxyUrl.description: Local proxy URL (required only when provider=qclaw). When configured, embedding requests are forwarded through this proxy and the original baseUrl is passed in the Remote-URL header

baseUrl.description: API base URL (required): the API endpoint for the selected provider

dimensions.description: Vector dimensions (required; must match the selected model)

conflictRecallTopK.description: Top-K recall count for conflict detection

maxInputChars.description: Maximum input text length for embeddings; longer input is truncated and a warning is logged (default 5000, suitable for most model token limits)

timeoutMs.description: Timeout for a single embedding API call in milliseconds; timed-out requests are aborted and not retried

recallTimeoutMs.description: Embedding timeout for the recall path in milliseconds; overrides timeoutMs. Users are waiting on this path, so a shorter value is recommended (for example, 3000)

captureTimeoutMs.description: Embedding timeout for the capture path in milliseconds; overrides timeoutMs. This runs in the background, so a longer value is acceptable (for example, 15000)

tcvdb.description: Tencent Cloud VectorDB configuration (effective only when storeBackend=tcvdb)

database.description: Database name (automatically generated from the instance ID by default when omitted)

alias.description: User-friendly alias (optional; used for identification in database.json)

caPemPath.description: Path to the CA certificate PEM file (used for HTTPS connections)

bm25.description: BM25 sparse-vector encoding settings (mainly for the tcvdb backend)

enabled.description: Whether to enable BM25 sparse-vector encoding
Whether to enable metrics reporting (outputs structured METRIC JSON through Gateway logs)

type.description: Reporting mode: local means structured JSON logs are emitted through the logger

llm.description: Standalone LLM configuration — when enabled, L1/L2/L3 extraction bypasses OpenClaw's built-in model and directly calls the specified OpenAI-compatible API. Disabled by default (uses the OpenClaw host model).

enabled.description: Whether to enable standalone LLM mode. When disabled, the OpenClaw host model is used.

model.description: Model name (for example, gpt-4o, deepseek-v3, claude-sonnet-4-6)

offload.description: Context Offload settings — a multi-level context compression system (independent switch, disabled by default)

enabled.description: Whether to enable Context Offload (disabled by default and does not affect Memory features)

model.description: LLM model used by Offload (format: provider/model). Uses the OpenClaw default model when omitted

forceTriggerThreshold.description: Number of accumulated tool pairs after which L1 is force-triggered

dataDir.description: Custom data directory (absolute path), defaults to ~/.openclaw/context-offload

maxPairsPerBatch.description: Maximum number of tool pairs per L1 batch

l2NullThreshold.description: Trigger L2 when the number of node_id=null entries in offload.jsonl reaches this threshold

l2TimeoutSeconds.description: Trigger L2 when this many seconds have elapsed since the previous L2 run

mildOffloadRatio.description: Mild compression trigger ratio (as a fraction of the context window)

mmdMaxTokenRatio.description: Token budget ratio for MMD injection

backendUrl.description: Backend service URL (for example, https://offload-api.example.com); when configured, L1/L1.5/L2/L4 use the backend
